reviews.review_description:
Simple graphics and gameplay, but awesome fun once you get the hang of things!
Really feels like your swinging about like a mad man!
Takes some time to practice to be good, but once at a fairly good level with the controls (i.e. getting them intuitive) you'll be flying like a crazy Spider dude! Yeeha Baby!
Also, sky diving like moments when you jump off a high building or cliff.
Geronimo!
Also, parkour style too!
Highly recommand, but go easy on those VR legs first in the beginning till you get used to the controls, then.....fly!
Cool to see how the sequel built off this solid foundation, and then Titan Isles took it to whole ‘nother level
A Vr Classic, timeless!  Absolutely Love Windlands!!  🫠🤙

Windlands takes a basic fantasy just about every little kid has - being spiderman/flying through the air - and absolutely delivers on it. The mechanics are simple, you can jump, shoot your grapple hook, lock the length of the cables, or give yourself more slack. At first you might even think the game is "clunky", but if you persist just a bit, soo you will realize the nuance of movement you can achieve with these simple mechanics is truly staggering. And the thing is, you WILL need to learn these mechanics to get a lot of the collectibles in the game as they can be tucked away in truly hard to reach places. Even the hardest collectibles are never frustrating to get to though because just interacting with the game's mechanics is a joy.

The only real downsides are that there are a couple of small bugs I encountered. A couple of times my controller inputs got stuck, and the subtitles on the voiceover in certain parts of the game aren't timed properly, but overall those are minor quibbles for what is a great game. I'm absolutely looking forward to seeing what these devs have cooked up in windlands 2.

I'm playing windlands 1 and regret buying the bundle now. The motion sickness is so intense and I don't usually have trouble with it not even with grappling games. The cage does nothing to ease this I don't know who thought that would help.
It's a great game idea, I like the ambience and everything, very simple controls.
Devs should look at other grappling games and see how they have achieved this movement style without causing sickness.

reviews.review_title: It appears to have been fixed.
reviews.review_description: Top favorite $20 ever spent on a video game. Have this on PSVR and had it on Vive years ago. In my opinion, it doesn't get better than this. It's neat to see the direction you went with Windlands 2 but a Windlands 3 made like the 1st Windlands would be phenomenal.

Swinging around with physics like Spider Man within search of The Missing Crystals as to find the Happy Ending. 

Similar to Journey and Flower. 

Woohoo!

Speed Run Time: 49 Minutes and 30 Seconds.
The devs need to not be meh and sweat eew kind of peepls

After playing this title for a few days I can confidently say it’s one of the best experiences in vr I’ve had. 

I have always had the dream of flying. Not in a plane, not with a glider or hovering, but actual flight. This game, once you get the hang of it, allows you to reach incredible speeds and heights. It also allows you to change settings in order to give a more realistic grappling experience. 

Overall, this is a hidden gem which has changed my perspective on the possibilities of VR development. Thank your for creating and maintaining this wonderful creation!

reviews.review_title: So happy to have this on Quest.
reviews.review_description: I have had this game on PSVR for ages and I was under the impression that it was too memory intensive to run on the quest. I was happy to be proven wrong, now I can play this wherever I want and it runs like a dream on the quest. It's a simple game without much of a storyline however, it's an environment that I love spending time in, its a very relaxing game yet still challenging enough especially on Normal and Hard mode. I highly recommend this game to everyone, the kind of game that thrives in VR. 😍👍🏻

Wind lands is the first game I ever wanted in vr. I got my first headset, original quest, in January 2020. I had been doing research for the games I wanted, and windlands was at the very top of my list. I didn’t, however, realize that windlands hadn’t yet been ported to quest. I thought they would’ve done it automatically as it seemed so straightforward to me since it was a simple but pretty style of game. I was so disappointed that I wasn’t able to get it, so I moved on. I checked every couple months to see if it had been ported yet, and it never was. But a couple weeks ago, I decided to scroll through side quest and there it was on the front page—finally. I downloaded it and the next day when I finally had some time, I played it and loved it so much. I love the journey of playing with the swinging and finding how to get up to those tricky spots and totems. I was so bummed when a couple days later I had already beat the game, and I haven’t played since as I wasn’t too interested in the timed mode. I hope we can get a port for windlands 2 to quest 2 if that’s possible, as this game deserves the second run. love you devs
Best parkour game on the store. I'm scared of heights so it does make my hands a bit sweaty lol but just adds to the thrill. Very fun.

Simple beautiful worlds you can swing around in. Collect the end of level gems to a perfect score, what more could you want? Well maybe a port of wind lands 2 please?
Great game! Yet another hidden gem.

I like the challenge of getting to higher heights. Exploring every nook and cranny. I did get a little queasy. But it was after I tried it with the cage. It reminds me of The Climb but you're relying on grappling hooks. Definitely worth it. Now trying to decide if I want the second one.

reviews.review_title: Unique VR title now on Quest
reviews.review_description:
This it the biggest gift developers can make to me. 
I've been dreaming about this game since I bought it on Steam a couple years ago. 
So Happy to revisit it again. This freedom feeling this game gives you is really unique and priceless.
Without Windlands there would not be Swarm, Yupitergrad and so on. Hope this game gets what it deserves on this platform. Don't miss it guys! I can't recommend it more.

Just took a chance and purchased this and it's stunning and so much fun!! Highly recommended.

Will add to this review once I have played it for  a longer period of time, but even if you play this to swing through the environments, it's a five star game!!!

Find us on Facebook - Group name "Ridoculus - Oculus Quest / SideQuest / App Lab"

This is a short and sweet game where you are swinging around like Spiderman, which is feels absolutely amazing, but is quite intense if you aren't used to VR.

The walking when you are on the ground is incredibly sluggish, which is annoying, but incentivizes swinging around. The swinging mechanics are a little janky at times, but incredibly fun and satisfying to master.

Could be my favorite VR game so far. This is a wonderful challenging exploration game. It’s beautiful and I love the big open world and figuring out how you’re going to get to that far pinnacle from where you are. It’s been hours of fun so far. If you like Daedalus￼ you’ll love this.

This game is an absolute blast, the physics are fun and challenging, it looks gorgeous, and has an interesting story so far! The motion is definitely a lot at times tho, and please do keep in mind I play with no comfort settings and smooth turn on all vr games, and have been playing daily for about a year. So the comfort settings might help, but I did find myself a wee nauseous after an hour. Other than that, 100% recommend!!!
